Option.Description: Use verbose for more debug/trace information
Display version

Command.Name: channels
Command.Description: Commands for channels

Command.Name: get
Command.Description: Operations related to retrieving channels.

Command.Name: all
Command.Description: Retrieve all channels

Option.Description: Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: euromap63
Command.Description: Get a EuroMap63 channel.

Option.Description: Channel Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: opcuaclient
Command.Description: Get a OPC UA Client channel.

Option.Description: Channel Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: create
Command.Description: Operations related to creating channels.

Command.Name: euromap63
Command.Description: Creates a EuroMap63 channel (if not exists).

Option.Description: Indicates whether or not to make the channel's diagnostic information available to an OPC application.
Requested Description
Requested Name
Sets the NonNormalizedFloatingPointHandling. Valid values are: ReplaceWithZero (default), Unmodified
Sets the OptimizationMethod. Valid values are: WriteAllValuesForAllTags, WriteOnlyLatestValueForNonBooleanTags, WriteOnlyLatestValueForAllTags (default)
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information
Specifies the ratio of write operations to read operations, based on one read per configurable number of writes.

Command.Name: opcuaclient
Command.Description: Creates a OPC UA Client channel (if not exists).

Option.Description: Indicates whether or not to make the channel's diagnostic information available to an OPC application.
Specify the maximum amount of time, in seconds the channel should wait to successfully connect after making a connect call
Requested Description
Endpoint Url for OPC UA Client Channel
Requested Name
Sets the NonNormalizedFloatingPointHandling. Valid values are: ReplaceWithZero (default), Unmodified
Password for OPC UA Client Channel
UserName for OPC UA Client Channel
Sets the OptimizationMethod. Valid values are: WriteAllValuesForAllTags, WriteOnlyLatestValueForNonBooleanTags, WriteOnlyLatestValueForAllTags (default)
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Sets the ServerMessageMode. Valid values are: None (default), Sign, SignAndEncrypt
Sets the ServerSecurityPolicy. Valid values are: None (default), Basic128Rsa15, Basic256, Basic256Sha256
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
Specifies the rate, in seconds, at which the channel attempts to reconnect if it fails to connect or becomes disconnected
Specifies the time, in minutes, between channel renewals
Specifies the maximum amount of time, in minutes, a session remains open without activity
Specifies the rate the client reads the server status state node
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information
Specifies the ratio of write operations to read operations, based on one read per configurable number of writes.

Command.Description: Delete channel

Option.Description: Channel Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: create
Command.Description: Operations related to creating tags and tag groups.

Command.Name: tag
Command.Description: Creates a tag (if not exists).

Option.Description: The tag address
ChannelName

Sets the Tag ClientAccessType. Valid values are: String, Boolean, Char, Byte, Short, Word, Long, DWord, Float, Double, Bcd, LBcd, Date, LLong, QWord, StringArray, BooleanArray, CharArray, ByteArray, ShortArray, WordArray, LongArray, DWordArray, FloatArray, DoubleArray, BcdArray, LBcdArray, DateArray, LLongArray, QWordArray

Sets the Tag DataType. Valid values are: String (default), Boolean, Char, Byte, Short, Word, Long, DWord, Float, Double, Bcd, LBcd, Date, LLong, QWord, StringArray, BooleanArray, CharArray, ByteArray, ShortArray, WordArray, LongArray, DWordArray, FloatArray, DoubleArray, BcdArray, LBcdArray, DateArray, LLongArray, QWordArray
Tag Description
DeviceName
Tag Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Specifies the poll interval, in milliseconds, for this tag.
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
Tag Groups indicating tree structure.
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: iot-agent
Command.Description: Commands for iot agents

Command.Name: mqtt-client
Command.Description: Operations related to MQTT Client Iot Agents

Command.Name: create
Command.Description: Create a mqtt-client iot agent (if not exists).

Option.Description: The client identifier used when connecting to the MQTT broker
Iot Agent Description
Indicates whether changes in quality should be ignored and not passed on
The number of tag events the gateway packages in a single transmission when using narrow format
The password to use when connecting to the MQTT broker
The username to use when connecting to the MQTT broker
Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Sets the format type for Publishing. Valid values are: Narrow (default), Wide
Specifies how messages should be formatted. Valid values are: Standard, Advanced (default)
The Quality of Service level (AtMostOnce=0, AtLeastOnce=1, ExactlyOnce=2)
Specifies the frequency, in milliseconds, at which the agent pushes data to the endpoint
Indicates if an initial update should be sent out on each tag when the Iot Agent starts up
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
The topic to publish data to
Defines the maximum amount of time, in seconds, allowed for a transaction to run
The URL of the MQTT broker endpoint (e.g., tcp://host:port or ssl://host:port)
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: get
Command.Description: Get a single mqtt-client iot agent.

Option.Description: Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: all
Command.Description: Get all mqtt-client iot agents.

Option.Description: Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: enable
Command.Description: Enable a mqtt-client iot agent (if exists).

Option.Description: Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: disable
Command.Description: Disable a mqtt-client iot agent (if exists).

Option.Description: Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: update
Command.Description: Update a mqtt-client iot agent (if exists).

Option.Description: The client identifier used when connecting to the MQTT broker
Iot Agent Description
Indicates whether the Iot Item is enabled
Indicates whether changes in quality should be ignored and not passed on
The number of tag events the gateway packages in a single transmission when using narrow format
The password to use when connecting to the MQTT broker
The username to use when connecting to the MQTT broker
Iot Agent Name (Not updateable, but required for updating the Iot Agent!)
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Sets the format type for Publishing. Valid values are: Narrow (default), Wide
Specifies how messages should be formatted. Valid values are: Standard, Advanced (default)
The Quality of Service level (AtMostOnce=0, AtLeastOnce=1, ExactlyOnce=2)
Specifies the frequency, in milliseconds, at which the agent pushes data to the endpoint
Indicates if an initial update should be sent out on each tag when the Iot Agent starts up
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
The topic to publish data to
Defines the maximum amount of time, in seconds, allowed for a transaction to run
The URL of the MQTT broker endpoint (e.g., tcp://host:port or ssl://host:port)
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: delete
Command.Description: Delete a mqtt-client iot agent (if exists).

Option.Description: Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: rest-client
Command.Description: Operations related to Rest Client Iot Agents

Command.Name: create
Command.Description: Create a rest-client iot agent (if not exists).

Option.Description: Iot Agent Description

Option.ClrType: System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary`2[[System.String, System.Private.CoreLib, Version=10.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=7cec85d7bea7798e],[System.String, System.Private.CoreLib, Version=10.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=7cec85d7bea7798e]]
Option.Description: The headers to send to url on each connection
Indicates whether changes in quality should be ignored and not passed on
The number of tag events the gateway packages in a single transmission when using narrow format
Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Sets the format type for Publishing. Valid values are: Narrow (default), Wide
Sets the HttpMethod for Publishing. Valid values are: Post (default), Put
Sets the media type for Publishing. Only valid when PublishMessageFormat is set to (Advanced). Valid values are: Json (default), Xml, XhtmlXml, TextPlain, TextHtml
Specifies how messages should be formatted. Valid values are: Standard, Advanced (default)
Specifies the frequency, in milliseconds, at which the agent pushes data to the endpoint
Indicates if an initial update should be sent out on each tag when the Iot Agent starts up
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
Defines the maximum amount of time, in seconds, allowed for a transaction to run
The URl of the endpoint to send data to
The password to use when calling the URl endpoint
The username to use when calling the URl endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: get
Command.Description: Get a single rest-client iot agent.

Option.Description: Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Indicates whether the Iot Item is enabled

Sets the HttpMethod for Publishing. Valid values are: Post (default), Put

Specifies how messages should be formatted. Valid values are: Standard, Advanced (default)
Specifies the frequency, in milliseconds, at which the agent pushes data to the endpoint
Indicates if an initial update should be sent out on each tag when the Iot Agent starts up
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
Defines the maximum amount of time, in seconds, allowed for a transaction to run
The URl of the endpoint to send data to
The password to use when calling the URl endpoint

Command.Name: iot-item
Command.Description: Commands for iot items

Command.Name: mqtt-client
Command.Description: Operations related to MQTT Client Iot Agent Iot Items

Command.Name: create
Command.Description: Create one or more iot items on a mqtt-client iot agent.

Option.Description: Specifies the DeadBand (%) when SendEveryScan is false (default: 0)
Indicates whether the Iot Item is enabled (default: true)
Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Specifies the frequency, in milliseconds, at which the iot item should be scanned (default: 10000)
Specifies if the tag should be published on every scan or only on data changes (default: false)
The server tag the Iot Item is pointing to
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: get
Command.Description: Get a single mqtt-client iot agent iot item.

Option.Description: Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
The server tag of the Iot Item
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: all
Command.Description: Get all mqtt-client iot agent iot items.

Option.Description: Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Name: enable
Command.Description: Enable a single mqtt-client iot agent iot item.

Option.Description: Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
The server tag of the Iot Item
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Description: Disable a single mqtt-client iot agent iot item.

Option.Description: Iot Agent Name
Password for Kepware server configuration endpoint
The server tag of the Iot Item
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information

Command.Description: Update a mqtt-client iot agent iot item (if exists).

Option.Description: Specifies the DeadBand (%) when SendEveryScan is false
Indicates whether the Iot Item is enabled

Specifies the frequency, in milliseconds, at which the iot item should be scanned
Specifies if the tag should be published on every scan or only on data changes
The server tag the Iot Item is pointing to (Not updateable, but required for updating the Iot Agent Iot Item!)
Server Url for Kepserver configuration endpoint
UserName for Kepware server configuration endpoint
Use verbose for more debug/trace information
